stem: Every networked device has both an IP address and a MAC address.
model_answer: A MAC address is 48 bits long and is usually shown as 6 pairs of 2-digit hexadecimal numbers separated by colons or hyphens (e.g. 3C:5A:37:1B:99:4F). The first half identifies the manufacturer and the second half is the unique serial number of the device.
review_notes: 2024 J277/01 Q2(a)(ii). '6 pairs of numbers' earns MP2+MP3; '4 pairs' earns only MP3.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Describe the format of a MAC address.

mark_points.text: 6 groups (12 hex digits) separated by colons or hyphens; 48 bits long; each group is 2 hex digits / 8 binary bits
first half is the manufacturer ID; second half is the device serial number
